description: Register transform.regex_match as a pure Transform action with explicit input and output schemas.
Implement regex_match inputs for text, pattern, optional flags, and optional requireMatch.
Implement regex_match output shape with matched, match, index, groups, and namedGroups.
Register transform.regex_extract as a pure Transform action with explicit input and output schemas.
Implement regex_extract inputs for text, pattern, optional flags, optional maxMatches, and optional requireMatch.
Implement regex_extract output shape with matched, count, first, and matches.
Support numbered capture groups in regex_match and regex_extract outputs.
Support JavaScript named capture groups in regex_match and regex_extract outputs where available.
Register transform.regex_replace as a pure Transform action with explicit input and output schemas.
Implement regex_replace inputs for text, pattern, optional flags, replacement, and optional replaceAll.
Implement regex_replace output shape with text and replacementCount.
Support or explicitly document JavaScript replacement tokens such as $1, $2, $<name>, and $$.
Add shared regex compilation helper that validates pattern syntax and returns actionable invalid-pattern errors.
Validate supported regex flags and reject unsupported or duplicate flags with actionable errors.
Enforce guardrails for maximum source text length, maximum pattern length, and maximum match count.
Handle zero-width global matches safely so extract and replace loops cannot hang.
Implement deterministic no-match behavior and requireMatch failure behavior.
Ensure regex actions are sideEffectful false and use engine-provided idempotency.
Expose regex action input and output fields through the workflow designer catalog.
Ensure regex transform outputs save through existing action.call saveAs behavior with no new assignment semantics.
Teach workflowDataContext/downstream reference building to expose stable regex output fields.
Optionally infer useful output fields from named capture groups when feasible without weakening runtime safety.
Render regex pattern, flags, replacement, maxMatches, and requireMatch inputs cleanly in the workflow designer.
Preserve and validate scoped saveAs paths such as payload.*, vars.*, and meta.* for regex outputs.
Improve Run Studio error display so regex action-level errors appear before generic wrapper errors.
Add Quick Ask guidance for regex action names, JavaScript regex syntax, flags, captures, replacements, saveAs, guardrails, and deterministic runtime behavior.
Preserve existing Quick Ask and AI Assistant entitlement gating while keeping regex actions available without AI entitlement.
Document guidance that saved regex captures may persist sensitive or secret-derived content into workflow state.
Keep existing workflows and current transform actions backward-compatible.
Add examples for common MSP parsing patterns such as incident IDs, hostnames, serial numbers, asset tags, and whitespace normalization.
